question_sentence: Which pop star was acquitted of tax fraud by a Spanish court?
Donald Trump announced plans to send 5,000 additional troops to which European country?
Which team won the English Premier League for the first time in 22 years?
Which US talk show came to an end this week after an 11-year run?
Which tech company laid off 10% of its workforce this week to focus more on AI?
Which country won the 2026 Eurovision Song Contest?
A parody political party in India named after what creature has amassed more social media followers than the country’s ruling party?
Which watch brand had to close stores because of customer demand for its new model?
A painting by which artist sold for $181 million at a New York auction house?
Which American reality competition accidentally revealed part of its ending before the final episode aired?
An amusement park is a great place to have some summer fun. Where was America’s first roller coaster built?
evidence: The first roller coaster in the US opened on Coney Island in Brooklyn, New York, in 1884. Coney Island is also home to Nathan's Hot Dog Eating Contest, the iconic July 4 competition held each year

question_sentence: You could also beat the summer heat inside a cool movie theater. Which movie is considered to be the first summer blockbuster?
evidence: Released on June 20, 1975, “Jaws” went on to become the first film to gross more than $100 million at the box office.

question_sentence: Hot dogs are a summer staple. Which city’s residents consume more hot dogs annually than those of any other city?
evidence: Los Angeles residents consume more hot dogs annually than any other city (about 28 million pounds), beating out New York and Chicago, according to the National Hot Dog and Sausage Council. Much of that appetite can be traced to the iconic “Dodger Dog,” the famously oversized hot dog that has become a staple at Los Angeles Dodgers games.

question_sentence: Watermelon is one of the season’s most refreshing treats. Roughly what percentage of it is actually water?
evidence: The average watermelon is about 92% water — and before you start tapping random melons at the grocery store, this video can help you pick the perfect one.

question_sentence: Memorial Day weekend is a great time to hit the beach. Which state is known as the Ocean State?
evidence: Surprising, right? The Ocean State is the official nickname of Rhode Island. While it’s the smallest state in the US, Rhode Island boasts some 400 miles of shoreline.

question_sentence: Speaking of states, state fair season is getting underway. Which fair is the largest in the US?
evidence: “Big Tex” welcomed more than 2 million visitors to the State Fair of Texas in 2025. And although the Iowa State Fair is not the largest, it is home to the famous butter cow sculpture.

question_sentence: Many travelers are also choosing cooler destinations — like Alaska, Scandinavia or coastal regions — to avoid extreme summer heat. Which region holds the record for the hottest summer temperature in history?
evidence: Death Valley is notorious for extreme heat. In 2024, the desert recorded its hottest summer on record, including nine consecutive days of temperatures reaching at least 125 degrees.

question_sentence: Lemonade is a classic summer drink. Which country is credited with inventing the refreshing beverage?
evidence: Most historians trace the origins of lemonade to ancient Egypt. “Kashkab,” a drink made with barley, mint, pepper and citron leaf, dates to 10th-century Egypt.

question_sentence: According to an antiquated social custom, what color clothing should you not wear after Labor Day, when summer comes to an end?
evidence: Fashion experts say the “rule” of not wearing white was one of many 19th-century style customs used to distinguish the upper and middle classes. White was tied to summer vacations — a privilege only a few could afford.

question_sentence: Finally, when does summer officially begin in the Northern Hemisphere?
evidence: Summer officially begins with the Summer Solstice on Sunday, June 21.
